name: Henrico County Public Schools
districtFact: Henrico County completely surrounds Richmond City, and many Henrico addresses list 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city — meaning families can sign a lease for what looks like a Richmond address and discover after move-in that their children are enrolled in Henrico County Public Schools, not Richmond City Public Schools.

about.title: About Henrico County Public Schools
about.content:
Henrico County Public Schools serves approximately 50,000 students across 74 schools in a county that wraps around Richmond City on its northern, eastern, and western sides. Henrico and Richmond are legally separate entities — separate county government, separate school board, separate school system — but their shared geography generates one of the most consistent misunderstandings for newcomers relocating to the Richmond area. A significant portion of Henrico addresses use 'Richmond, VA' as their mailing city. Newcomers can sign a lease for what appears to be a Richmond address and discover only after signing that their children fall within HCPS attendance zones rather than Richmond City Public Schools. The confusion runs in both directions: households specifically seeking HCPS sometimes search for homes 'in Richmond,' inadvertently excluding some of the county's best neighborhoods from consideration. School assignment is determined by Henrico's attendance zone maps, not by what appears in the mailing address. Verify any address at henricoschools.us before finalizing housing.

The county spans from the dense suburban corridors of Short Pump and Glen Allen in the west to the more rural and historically distinct communities of Varina and Sandston in the east. Most corporate relocations land in western Henrico, where a concentration of newer construction, multiple elementary school zones within a compact area, and direct access to I-64 and I-295 make it the default starting point for out-of-state arrivals. The eastern part of the county has lower housing costs, more established neighborhoods, and sits closer to Richmond International Airport — which is located within Henrico's boundaries, not the City of Richmond.

about.title: What to Expect in the First School Year
about.content:
The 2026–2027 school year introduces a staggered kindergarten and pre-K start, new for HCPS. On August 24, half of all incoming pre-K and kindergarten students attend their first day; the remaining new-to-school students begin August 25, with all students together by August 26. Students in Grades 1–5, Grade 6, and Grade 9 also begin August 24. Students in Grades 7–8 and Grades 10–12 begin August 25. Households with children at multiple grade levels need to check which specific date applies to each child — the staggered structure means some siblings may have different first days.

Specialty program applications — Governor's School programs, advanced academics pathways, International Baccalaureate programs, and career and technical education offerings — open in the fall and close in the winter. Households arriving between September and December are within the application window. Those arriving in January or later will typically wait until the next enrollment cycle. For mid-year enrollees, HCPS can generally place students in a home school within days of a completed application, but access to competitive specialty programs is constrained by seats already committed.

Virginia observes Good Friday as a school holiday, and HCPS follows this practice. In 2026–2027, Good Friday falls March 26 — the Friday immediately before Spring Break. The combined window runs from March 26 through April 4, giving students approximately 10 consecutive days off including both surrounding weekends. Households booking spring travel should plan around this full window rather than Spring Break week alone. Thanksgiving Break in 2026–2027 covers November 25–27 only. Monday and Tuesday of Thanksgiving week are school days — different from some neighboring Virginia districts that observe the full week.

planningTips: Address verification matters here more than in most metro areas. Many Henrico addresses list 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city — school division depends on the attendance zone, not the city in your address. Verify at henricoschools.us before committing to any housing.

Good Friday (March 26) is a school holiday in HCPS and falls the Friday before Spring Break. The combined off-school window — March 26 through April 4, including weekends — is approximately 10 days. Book spring travel based on this full window.

Thanksgiving Break in 2026–2027 covers November 25–27 only, not the full week. Monday November 23 and Tuesday November 24 are school days. Households planning Thanksgiving travel that requires earlier departure should account for the shorter break window.

Specialty program applications (Governor's School, IB, advanced academics) open in the fall and close mid-winter. Households arriving between September and December should contact HCPS Student Services immediately — don't wait for spring open house events.

The staggered kindergarten and pre-K start is new for 2026–2027. Incoming kindergartners will be assigned either August 24 or August 25 as their first day — confirm which date applies to your child directly with the assigned school before the first week of school.

districtFaqs.q: What school district is Short Pump in?
districtFaqs.a: Short Pump is in Henrico County and is served by Henrico County Public Schools. Short Pump is not a separate municipality — it's an unincorporated community within Henrico County. Students in Short Pump attend HCPS schools based on their attendance zone. Many Short Pump addresses list 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city, which leads some newcomers to assume they're enrolling in Richmond City Public Schools. School division is determined by Henrico's attendance zone boundaries, not the mailing city. Verify your address at henricoschools.us.

districtFaqs.q: What school district is Glen Allen in?
districtFaqs.a: Glen Allen is in Henrico County and is served by Henrico County Public Schools. Like Short Pump, Glen Allen is an unincorporated community within the county — not a separate city or municipality. Some Glen Allen addresses may show 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city. School assignment follows Henrico's attendance zone maps regardless of what appears in the mailing address. Use the address lookup tool at henricoschools.us to confirm which school serves your specific address.

districtFaqs.q: Is Henrico County Public Schools the same as Richmond City Public Schools?
districtFaqs.a: No. Henrico County Public Schools and Richmond City Public Schools are completely separate school divisions with different school boards, budgets, and academic calendars. Henrico County is an independent county that surrounds Richmond City on three sides. Because many Henrico addresses list 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city, newcomers frequently assume they are in Richmond City Public Schools and find out after move-in that they're actually in HCPS. Confirm which division serves your address at henricoschools.us before making any housing or enrollment decisions.

districtFaqs.q: Why does my Henrico County address say 'Richmond, VA'?
districtFaqs.a: The US Postal Service assigns mailing city designations based on postal delivery routes, not political boundaries. Large portions of Henrico County — particularly in the Short Pump, Glen Allen, and eastern Henrico areas near the airport — use 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city even though those addresses are clearly in Henrico County, not the City of Richmond. This is a postal convention, not a jurisdictional one. School enrollment is based on Henrico County's attendance zone boundaries. If you're unsure which school division serves your address, use the zone lookup tool at henricoschools.us.

districtFaqs.q: When does Henrico County school start in 2026?
districtFaqs.a: The 2026–2027 school year begins August 24, 2026, with a new staggered start structure for pre-K and kindergarten students. Half of all incoming pre-K and kindergarten students begin August 24; the other half begin August 25. Students in Grades 1–5, Grade 6, and Grade 9 also begin August 24. Students in Grades 7–8 and Grades 10–12 begin August 25. All students are in school together starting August 26, 2026. The school year ends June 4, 2027.

districtFaqs.q: When is Spring Break in Henrico County?
districtFaqs.a: Spring Break in 2026–2027 runs March 29–April 2, 2027. Good Friday (March 26) is observed as a school holiday in HCPS and falls the Friday before Spring Break begins. The combined break window — Good Friday, the weekend, and Spring Break week — runs March 26 through April 4, giving students approximately 10 consecutive days off. Households booking spring travel should plan around the full Good Friday through end-of-Spring Break window, not just the five Spring Break days.

districtFaqs.q: Does Henrico County observe Good Friday as a school holiday?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Good Friday is a school holiday in Henrico County Public Schools, consistent with longstanding Virginia practice across most of the state's public school divisions. In 2026–2027, Good Friday falls March 26, the Friday before Spring Break begins on March 29. The combined window from Good Friday through the end of Spring Break gives students approximately 10 consecutive days off from March 26 through April 4. Households relocating from states where Good Friday is a regular school day should expect a noticeably longer spring break than they're used to.

districtFaqs.q: Does Henrico have the same calendar as Chesterfield County?
districtFaqs.a: Not exactly. Henrico County Public Schools and Chesterfield County Public Schools are separate school divisions with independently set calendars. In 2026–2027, both begin August 24 and end around the same time in early June. However, specific dates for teacher workdays, early-release days, and Thanksgiving Break differ. Henrico's Thanksgiving Break runs November 25–27; Chesterfield follows a different schedule. Households coordinating childcare across both divisions should download each district's official calendar separately from henricoschools.us and oneccps.org rather than assuming the two systems align.

districtFaqs.q: How does the staggered kindergarten start work in Henrico County?
districtFaqs.a: Beginning with the 2026–2027 school year, Henrico County Public Schools introduced a phased start for incoming pre-K and kindergarten students. Half of the new cohort begins August 24; the remaining students begin August 25. The approach gives kindergarten teachers time to work with smaller groups of new students before the full class is together. Parents will be notified by their assigned school which date their child is scheduled to attend. All students are in class together starting August 26. This staggered structure is new for 2026–2027 and does not apply to students in Grades 1 and above.

districtFaqs.q: What magnet or specialty programs does HCPS offer?
districtFaqs.a: Henrico County Public Schools offers specialty programs including regional Governor's School programs for high school students, IB (International Baccalaureate) courses at select schools, advanced academics pathways in middle and high school, and career and technical education programs through the Henrico Regional Governor's School. Application timelines vary by program, but most open in the fall and close in the winter — households arriving between September and December are typically within the application window. Households arriving in January or later generally need to wait until the following academic year. Contact HCPS Student Services for current program availability and application status.

districtFaqs.q: How do snow days work in Henrico County?
districtFaqs.a: When winter weather threatens, HCPS may cancel school, delay the start by one or two hours, or activate emergency remote learning days. The district builds makeup days into the calendar; if closures exceed those days, the school year may extend past the June 4 end date. Richmond typically sees one to four meaningful winter weather events per year — fewer than Northern Virginia, more than most Southern states. Two-hour delays are common for ice; full closures happen when overnight accumulation makes roads and bridges unsafe. Sign up for HCPS emergency alerts at henricoschools.us to receive real-time notifications.

livingHere.intro: Henrico County surrounds Richmond City but operates independently — its own school board, its own calendar, and its own community identity, even when the mailing address says 'Richmond, VA.' The county's geography spans far enough that Short Pump and Varina feel like different places, and most households new to the area don't realize how much variety the county contains until they start comparing school zones.

highlights.label: The 'Richmond Address' Problem
highlights.detail:
Henrico County completely surrounds Richmond City, and a large share of Henrico's residential addresses — particularly in Short Pump, Glen Allen, and parts of eastern Henrico near the airport — use 'Richmond, VA' as the mailing city. This creates a consistent problem for families relocating from out of state. They search for housing in 'Richmond,' find a neighborhood they like, sign a lease, and then discover only after move-in that their children's school assignment is through Henrico County Public Schools — not Richmond City Public Schools. The two systems are completely independent, with different school boards, different calendars, different attendance zones, and very different academic profiles. The confusion runs in both directions: households specifically seeking HCPS schools sometimes eliminate the best Henrico neighborhoods from their search because the address says 'Richmond' — and then wind up in a different county altogether.

The practical consequences can be significant. A family that relocates expecting Richmond City Public Schools may find their children assigned to HCPS, which has different magnet program structures, different calendar dates, and different school choice deadlines. A family that wanted HCPS may have signed a lease in Richmond City without realizing it. Neither outcome is easy to fix mid-year. The first step for any household arriving in the Richmond metro is to run every candidate address through the HCPS attendance zone lookup at henricoschools.us before committing to any lease or purchase. What the mailing address says and which school system it belongs to are two entirely separate questions.

highlights.label: Short Pump — Where Corporate Relocations Land
highlights.detail: Short Pump, in western Henrico along US-250 and West Broad Street, is where most out-of-state households end up first. Newer construction, multiple elementary school zones in a compact area, and direct access to I-64 and I-295 make it the default recommendation from corporate relocation services and real estate agents. The tradeoff: in-demand elementary zones fill early. Households arriving after July find capacity limited at some schools, with sibling priority and existing attendance commitments taking available seats before general enrollment opens. Arriving before June provides more options and time to compare zones before committing.

highlights.label: Richmond International Airport (RIC)
highlights.detail: RIC is in eastern Henrico County — not the City of Richmond — about 20–35 minutes from most residential areas depending on traffic. It serves as the departure airport for most HCPS family travel, with direct service to major East Coast hubs. The three busiest booking windows track the HCPS calendar: the Thanksgiving stretch (November 25–27 in 2026–2027 — only three days, not the full week), December Winter Break, and Spring Break in late March. Because RIC has fewer nonstop routes than Dulles or Charlotte Douglas, peak-season seats fill faster than many households expect. Reserve flights within a few days of the HCPS calendar release each spring for Spring Break travel in particular.

highlights.label: Winter Weather and School Delays
highlights.detail: Richmond sits in a transitional climate zone where winter weather is real but unpredictable year to year. The most common closure scenario is freezing rain overnight — roads ice faster than snow accumulates, and HCPS typically calls delays or closures when bridges and overpasses are unsafe even when neighborhoods look clear. Some winters bring three or four meaningful disruptions; others pass with minimal impact. HCPS uses a combination of built-in makeup days and emergency remote learning days to make up lost instruction time, and the June 4 end date may shift in a heavier snow year. The Deep South transplant will find Richmond winters more disruptive than expected; the Northern Virginia or Northeast transplant will find them noticeably milder. Full closures are less common here than in NoVA, but more frequent than in most Southern cities.

highlights.label: Innsbrook and Capital One — Corporate Relocation Context
highlights.detail: The Innsbrook Corporate Center in western Henrico is one of Virginia's largest office parks, housing financial services, technology, and professional services firms. Capital One and Dominion Energy both have significant operations in or adjacent to the corridor. The result is a steady volume of professionals on corporate relocation packages — most of whom land in the Short Pump and Glen Allen residential zones that border Innsbrook directly.

highlights.label: Richmond City Institutions — Accessible from Henrico
highlights.detail: Henrico households have access to Richmond City's cultural infrastructure without enrolling in Richmond City Public Schools. The Virginia Museum of Fine Arts, Science Museum of Virginia, Children's Museum of Richmond, and Lewis Ginter Botanical Garden are all within 15–30 minutes of most Henrico neighborhoods. HCPS elementary and middle schools run field trips to these institutions as part of their curriculum — the Science Museum and VMFA appear on many elementary field trip schedules. Virginia Commonwealth University, in Richmond City, hosts public events and academic programs accessible to the broader metro. For households used to urban amenities, Henrico offers suburban living with meaningful Richmond City access a short drive away.

highlights.label: Good Friday and Spring Break — The 10-Day Window
highlights.detail: Virginia observes Good Friday as a school holiday. In 2026–2027, Good Friday is March 26, the Friday before Spring Break begins on March 29. The combined window — Good Friday, the weekend, and Spring Break week (March 29–April 2) — gives students approximately 10 consecutive days off from March 26 through April 4. For households accustomed to states where Good Friday is a regular school day, the spring break window is longer than expected.

relatedDistricts.name: Chesterfield County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: The other major suburban school division in the Richmond metropolitan area. Chesterfield surrounds Richmond to the south and west, while Henrico surrounds it to the north and east. Households comparing both counties when deciding where to live in the Richmond metro will find similar suburban profiles but different calendars, boundary structures, and school assignments.

relatedDistricts.name: Fairfax County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Virginia's largest school division and the primary comparison point for households relocating within the state. Federal employees, defense contractors, and others moving from Northern Virginia to the Richmond area frequently compare Fairfax and Henrico academic offerings, calendar structures, and AP course availability.

relatedDistricts.name: Prince William County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: A large Northern Virginia district that households sometimes compare against Henrico when weighing the Richmond corridor against the DC metro. The two districts serve different metros but are both mid-to-large Virginia suburban systems, and the comparison comes up frequently for households considering a move away from Northern Virginia.
